title: BUbreakingSectionTwo
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbreakingSectionTwo, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: Big Tech execs playing ‘Russian roulette’ in the AI arms race could risk human extinction, warns top researcher
items.description: Allowing companies free rein on AI is bringing on a new level of existential risk, Stuart Russell argues.

items.title: Colorado Democrats Push to Rip Up 2028 House Map for an Extra Edge
items.description: A proposed ballot measure to give Democrats more seats for the 2028 and 2030 elections signals that the nation’s gerrymandering fight is likely to per...

items.title: Mark Cuban slams the NBA for focusing on tanking and ignoring the real issue for fans: ‘It should worry more about pricing fans out of games’
items.description: The Dallas Mavericks, with Cuban as a stakeholder, raised prices nearly 9% ahead of the 2025-2026 season, despite fans’ ire over trading All-Star Luka...

items.title: E.P.A. Plans to Loosen Mercury Rules for Coal Plants, Documents Show
items.description: Senior officials at the Environmental Protection Agency are expected to announce the move on Friday, according to people briefed on the matter.

items.title: High flyer: Palantir CEO Alex Karp spent $17.2 million on private jets in 2025, filing reveals
items.description: One out of every 3.5 days was spent in the air for the Palantir CEO during 2025, filing shows.

items.title: Buffalo Wild Wings wins legal debate after a customer discovered his boneless wings contained no real wing meat at all
items.description: A judge ruled in favor of Buffalo Wild Wings for a March 2023 case, saying the customer’s argument “has no meat on its bones.”

items.title: Leslie Wexner Says Epstein ‘Conned’ Him, but Democrats Are Skeptical
items.description: House Republicans skipped a deposition with Leslie Wexner, the retail billionaire, and Democrats said his answers were not credible.

items.title: Mark Zuckerberg Takes the Stand in Social Media Addiction Trial
items.description: Meta’s chief executive said users spent a lot of time on Instagram because of its value, as he was grilled about child safety issues in front of a jur...

items.title: Jake Paul says a chance meeting with Sam Altman at Trump’s inauguration led to an OpenAI investment and a crash course in ruthless 15-minute meetings
items.description: Paul said the OpenAI CEO showed him “time is the most valuable thing.”

items.title: Mamdani to Reinstitute Homeless Encampment Sweeps

items.title: South Sudan Appoints Dead Man to Election Panel, as Political Crisis Grows

items.title: Alberto Tomba Was an ’80s and ’90s Icon. The Olympics Have Brought Him Back.

items.title: From 1966: Anna Akhmatova, Leading Soviet Poet, Is Dead
items.description: She was a towering figure in Soviet literature who was once silenced in a Stalinist literary purge.

items.title: From 2009: Millvina Dean, Titanic’s Last Survivor, Dies at 97
items.description: She was just 9 weeks old when she was lowered to safety in a mail sack, surviving the worst maritime disaster in history.

items.title: An Antarctic Expedition Ends in New Zealand
items.description: But stay tuned: We’ve still got more to share about this Antarctic expedition, and the next ones scientists are already planning.
